figures.caption: Figure 3: Auto-associative topology with 6 input/output dimensions (blue). The 15-node latent space is shown in red.
Figure 4: Left: Recurrent network topology, storing autoassociations A→ A. Right: Feed-forward network with two inputs for heteroassociations A→ B.
Figure 5: SHR storage capacity as a function of memory density ρ for hyperparameters N = 1, 000 and P = 10.
Figure 6: Retrieval accuracy as a function of memory density ρ.
Figure 7: Memory capacity to store associations with SHR population P = 10, as a function of dimension N .
Figure 8: Retrieval accuracy with 3 random elements dropped, as a function of memory density ρ.
Figure 9: Retrieval accuracy with 15 random elements added, as a function of memory density ρ.
Figure 10: Probability of false-positive responses to random queries, as a function of memory density ρ. This simulation uses heteroassociations with N = 1, 000 and P = 10.
Figure 11: Expected size of a superposition as a function of the number of bundled SHRs, assuming N = 1, 000 and P = 10.
Figure 12: Usable bundle size at which false-positive matches are unlikely, as a function of dimensionality N . This simulation uses a fixed SHR population P = 10.

Figure 16: Average number of iterations needed to extract one token from a bundle, as a function of the number of bundled tokens. This is simulated with hyperparameters N = 2, 000 and P = 10. The results are consistent with Miller’s magical number seven, plus or minus two.
Figure 48: A reservoir architecture incorporating bigram encoding and a dual-delay reservoir.
Figure 49: Sample digits from the MNIST Handwritten Digit Database
Figure 54: A similarity matrix of 9 words, computed as the ARI between their semantic fields.
Figure 57: Random walk on a knowledge graph using the complement update rule.
Figure 58: Random walk on a knowledge graph using the difference update rule.
Figure 59: Dendritic on/off switches as the mechanism of topological plasticity.
Figure 60: Cognitive performance as a response to a neuromodulation factor that corresponds to stochastic subsampling in memory retrieval.
